finding: Z-score lookback now uses 2-year warmup (2018-2019) giving ~750 bars by trade_start. Full 252-slope window achieved.
Quintile method switched to pd.qcut equivalent with linear interpolation. Verified against first 5 trades.
Stop now uses resting sell limit at stop_price. KITE matching engine fills at exact stop_price. Eliminates the largest P&L divergence source.
recommendation: No action needed. Excellent fix — this was the $15M gap.

finding: md.L1.low check no longer needed since stop is a resting limit order. KITE's matching engine handles the fill.
recommendation: Confirm that the resting limit stays active across days and isn't cancelled overnight by KITE.

finding: Day counting confirmed: CRM 2020-01-10 → 2020-02-10 matches exactly. Increment guard in place.
Entry fill at open+2s vs exact open. ~0.1% difference.
recommendation: Acceptable. Log fills for post-hoc comparison.

finding: Exit fill at close-30s vs exact close. ~0.05% difference.
Cost model: spec deducts 5 bps each way explicitly. KITE P&L reflects actual fills with no additional deduction. Makes P&L comparison ambiguous.
recommendation: Must clarify: when comparing KITE P&L to spec's $88.2M are you comparing gross or net? The difference is ~$2M across 2082 trades. Add explicit cost tracking or document which comparison is being made.

finding: Split handling via price ratio detection and buffer re-seed. ~5 symbols affected with temporary shorter lookback.
recommendation: Good pragmatic fix. Log split events to confirm all 5 expected splits are detected.

finding: ATR14 seed matches to 4 decimal places (MRK 1.0534 vs 1.053433).
body_pct first 3 trades match. Split-neutral ratio confirmed.
recommendation: Verify 10 trades in quick test as planned.

finding: Halted symbols excluded from quintile sort. Positions keep tracking. Matches spec.

Resting stop limit order placed on days_held==2. What if the stock gaps below stop on entry day (days_held==1)? Spec says stop check starts day 1 AFTER entry which matches. But confirm no edge case where the stock immediately gaps below stop and the limit isn't placed yet.
recommendation: On entry day the stop limit is NOT placed. If stock gaps down 10% on entry day the stop doesn't fire until day 2 when the limit is placed. This matches our spec (we also don't check on entry day). Just confirm this is intentional.

finding: Stop limit order cancellation before time exit. Row 58 says order.cancel(sym) before algo_sell. What if the cancel fails or has latency? Could end up with both a stop fill AND a time exit fill on the same day.
recommendation: Add a check: if position already closed by stop fill (detected in on_fill) then do NOT submit time exit sell. The on_fill handler should set a flag that the exit timer checks.

finding: Dedup check (row 17) compares last stored close vs new bar close. What if two consecutive days have the same close? Would trigger false dedup and miss a bar.
recommendation: Use timestamp comparison instead of close price comparison for dedup. Two consecutive days with identical closes is rare but possible (especially for low-priced stocks).

finding: Buffer re-seed on split (row 72) discards entire accumulated buffer and reads 252 KITE bars. But KITE's retroactive adjustment may not perfectly match Polygon's adjustment for older bars.
recommendation: This is acceptable and unavoidable. The z-scores will differ slightly for ~60 days post-split for the 5 affected symbols. Document as known limitation.

finding: No mention of reconciliation checkpoints. The spec provides cumulative P&L at specific dates (2020-06-11 = +$7.6M etc). Should verify these after the full run.
recommendation: After backtest completes compare KITE's cumulative P&L at: 2020-06-30 (+$9.3M) 2020-12-31 (+$23.8M) 2021-12-31 (+$33.1M) 2022-12-31 (+$33.0M). Within ±25% at each checkpoint = PASS.
